description: A form of logical inference which starts with observations and seeks the most plausible explanation. In the toolbox, this relates to generating hypotheses from data artifacts.
A method of statistical inference using Bayes' theorem to update probabilities as evidence accumulates.
Reduction of complexity while preserving essential informational structure; distillation of reality into models.
The number of variables or parameters in a model.
The study of knowledge, its origin, scope, and justification.
A practical problem-solving method that is not guaranteed optimal but is efficient.
The process of deriving conclusions from premises or evidence.
A simplified representation of a system or phenomenon.
The study of what exists; defines the entities in a domain.
Identification of regularities and structure in data.
The total set of phenomena independent of representation.
Measure comparing meaningful signal to background noise.
Any method or mechanism used to construct, represent, or validate knowledge.

An epistemic prior (aka inductive bias) is a modeling practical assumption (not affirmation) about the underlying segment of reality that can informs the selection, solution, and validation of the resulting epistemic artifact, as well as the epistemic tools (representational forms, etc.) used to derive it.
An externalized construct that embodies a representation of reality.
Any operation performed to construct, transform, or evaluate knowledge.
The study of failure modes in epistemic processes.
